channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave — 160 replies — 640.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave

Open thread • Replies: 160 • Updated: 2026-05-03 23:26 UTC

OP

https://x.com/dailymail/status/2049618941043949691?s=46

A white woman JP Morgan Executive (mega international bank) drugged and raped a married man before taking pictures of him to use as blackmail.

She would force him to take drugs like viagra so she could fuck him longer. She started buying him gifts which as sex toys including anal ones which she forced him to use and got him a position where she had direct control over him and he acted as her assistant. She also pegged him.

He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.

She was caught when police investigated her for ties to Trump and Epstein with her blackmailing the employee being unclassified.

Early replies (first 3)

62172413
>>62172412

62172414
>>62172413
kek

62172420
>>62172413
post her tits are they big and nice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176996
>>62175827
When the bank says there's nothing there that's pretty bad.
Also he's Indian so it's clearly fanfiction.

62177044
>>62174634
>>62174688
Basically this, it was a jeet writing his own fanfiction on his laptop but he forgot to delete them before they were checked out by LEO

62177061
>>62172448
dudes, i've never felt so abandoned by god as i do in this moment

62177089
>>62172412
what the fuck is wrong with this faggot? if this shit happened me i'd quit my job to solely be her live in fuckmeat. i'd do all the weird nasty shit she'd be into as well like huffing her farts in a gas mask.

>>During the second encounter, Hajdini allegedly ordered him to suck her toes, pushed him to the ground and sat on his face, berating him when he was unable to sustain arousal.

I don't even like toes either dude but you'd bet your fucking ass i'd be cleaning every grain of dirt off of those fucking things and smelling the fuck out of them. fucking loser faggot

62177151
lmao this is exactly how Indians think white women are. It's so obvious that it's jeet delusions

62177156
>>62177089
This is all pajeet bullshit lies. Why would you trust anyone who shits in the street.

62177231
>>62172412
Aaaaaaand it turns out the jeet literally made it all up.

62179620
>>62177089
>I don't even like X but I would do X
get your T tested, see if you're able to do a flip off a bridge.

62179655
>>62174634
>show bobs and vagene
let a man dream

62180085
>>62172412
It's fake.
How do I know?
1. No one in the US calls Indians Asians.
Let alone calling an Indian guy "little brown Asian boy"
2. He claims to have a Asian wife, but doesn't in reality.
It's buck breaking 2.0 SAARS edition

That women will forever be blasted by that fake civil suit.

62180177
fake and gay

62180263
>>62173170
You were right.

62180643
>>62174634
This sounds very fake.
I suggest that alternatively
>Jeet with wife sexually harassed his woman boss
>Is denied and loses 3000 izzat
>Makes up this story to recoup his losses

62180818
>>62176986
It's AI you mong

62180821
>>62173364
>pending

FFS leave her alone.


>>62176674

Is that real?

62180823
>>62172454
Literally younger jack ma with a wig

62180838
>>62172412
Whats the deal with her, is she married/kids?
Is she really Albanian?

62180849
>>62174807
>western women in japan or thailand getting no dates

lol. they would 100% get tons of dates from asian men who want a blonde gf. but they are probably just picky bitches who want for a 10/10 western expat chad

62180851
>>62172412
>He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.
Yeah, nobody else believes him either.

>saar my benis is so yuge that white bitch demand it from me saar

>ties to Trump and Epstein
Then why was she going after a guy of legal age?

62180859
>>62180849
>they are probably just picky bitches
Yep. I lived in Taiwan for most of the last 20 years, and the two things every white male always heard from Western female expats was (1) they can't get dates because all the white guys have yellow fever, and (2) "eww the local guys have tiny dicks and don't make enough money to date me!"

In all fairness the Taiwanese guys do have tiny dicks tho.

62180991
>>62180975
not funny

62180994
>>62172412
THIS MAN IS NOT INDIAN HE IS PAKISTAN PLEASE STOP WITH THE SPREADING OF MISINFORMATION IT IS VERY DAMAGING

62181004
>>62180991
Get sprayed, bug

62181132
>>62175811
Looking forward to the defamation lawsuit

62183872
>>62180994
All brown fuck off.

62183970
>>62183872
He's not indian, he's Nepalese.

62184054
I have not had a extensive look into the story yet.
But here is a funny aspect.

Her name Hajdini
originates from:
The surname Hajdini is of Albanian origin, rooted in the Balkans,
It is derived from the word hajdin,
which historically referred to an outlaw or brigand

They were outlaws used in europe to fight Arabs who wanted to conquer Europe
(A different way to write it is Hajduk
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hajduk
The etymology of the word hajduk is unclear. One theory is that hajduk was derived from the Turkish word haidut or haydut 'bandit',
... because the Turkish word haidut or haydut is adapted from the Hungarian hajtó or hajdó
Other spellings in English include ajduk, haydut, haiduk, haiduc, hayduck, and hayduk.

It is interesting because aren't there posts where she allegedly refers
to his arab origin ...

62184088
>>62180851
>Yeah, nobody else believes him either.
It would at least be interesting to ask this question, and have a look at who profits from throwing her out.

62185236
GOONER
FAN
I
C
T
I
O
N

item.title: Is LINK going to surpass its ATH? — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is LINK going to surpass its ATH?

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-03 23:24 UTC

OP

Is LINK going to surpass its ATH?

Early replies (first 3)

62181449
>>62181442
yes it will wick above the ath within the next 12 months then will not return to $50 until 2031

62181450
>>62181449
I thought it was supposed to be in March what happened

62181456
>>62181442
probably... but a future where link is $50+ is one in which no sane man would want to live. careful what you wish for anon.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181459
Yes, $1488 EOY

62181460
>>62181442
>is stink gonna break its ATH when it cant even break 10 dollars?
uh, no. lmao

62181681
>>62181442
No. It made a lower high this cycle and there will be further dilution of token supply in future cycles as token dumps continue.

62181690
Hasn't Sergei been in hiding since his butt buddy left SWIFT?

62181761
I awoke from a dream this morning. The LINK price had suddenly rocketed and the board was going berzerk. The news was even pinned to the 'log by the jannies.
This is my first time posting in months, felt I had to share it. For me, everything is riding on LINK.

62181847
>>62181761
keep the faith only 5 more years

62181866
>>62181761
Interesting. I just dreamed about turning into a girl and catching turtles in a parking lot

62181990
>>62181681
That movie theater is laid-out!

Wow!

62182023
>>62181866
I had a dream I was dancing in the sky while human by the killers was playing

62182050
>>62181442
It's one of the only cryptocurrencies I don't think will.

62182054
>>62181442
dunno but i like your frog

62182061
>>62182023
For some reason I never dreamed songs that already exist.
My brain just makes up generic songs

62182137
>>62181761
Right there with ya buddy. We're all gonna make it.

62182141
It will end the year at $250. I can feel it in my bones

62182143
>>62181442
That is not for you to know. Your fate is to deal in the real world.

62182147
>>62181442
weak community.

id rather invest in xrp. just for the deleuded comunnity alone.

link has no pumpamntals, only dumpamentals.


i think link will hit 13 and then go back to under a dollar for the rest of eternity,

so anyone looking to get in , get in now but sell that shit at 13

62182148
>>62182141
is there an inverse-bonewhisperer ETF?

62184164
>>62181681
that's not what is really happening though, look at the chart

62184166
tokenization is increasing, those aum people need chainlink

62184240
>>62182141
this has been posted by many over the past 8 years so this MUST be the year

62185160
don't sell now marines, please!

62185230
>>62185160
this (but ironically)

item.title: There are people who are willing to risk 100% of their money in defi protocols for 3% APY, hoping that increasingly adva… — 19 replies — 41.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are people who are willing to risk 100% of their money in defi protocols for 3% APY, hoping that increasingly adva…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-03 23:03 UTC

OP

There are people who are willing to risk 100% of their money in defi protocols for 3% APY, hoping that increasingly advanced AI models won't find any exploits in the lending protocol, in any of the collateral assets or bridges that utilize the collateral assets, even though that has been happening on an almost monthly basis for the past 2-3 years

Early replies (first 3)

62184173
>>62184157
Some of this money is from regions where they have no access to better financial products such as US stocks. Other is from people who have a massive distrust of the trad financial system and so would take that risk to avoid having money in the bank.

62184211
>>62184173
Stablecoins can be held in cold wallets, since 3% yield does not justify all the risks.

US stocks can be traded on hyperliquid, which is arguably much safer than defi protocols since there are numerous attack vectors besides the protocol itself that leaves your capital at risk (see the recent kelpdao/rsETH incident almost bankrupting AAVE despite AAVE not doing anything wrong)

62184237
>>62184157
Why is Bam’s appearance so unsettling? I guess he looks crazed and violent, but seems pretty chill irl.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184487
>>62184237
he's only 23 in this pic

62184621
>>62184157
half the global population is dumber than the average person you see on the street

62184650
>>62184621
When you take the global population-weighted average into account (not the politically correct bullshit where every country has a 100 IQ average), the global average is closer to 85.

Considering I live somewhere with an average IQ of 102, approximately 87% of the global population is dumber than the average person I see on the street

62184682
>>62184157
you're a retarded nigger

62184685
>>62184682
Did you miss the part where DeFi protocols had to unite and donate to AAVE to keep them from imploding, and that you couldn't withdraw your stablecoins for like 5 days because there was no liquidity left?

That was after a measly $300M~ bridge exploit. Imagine if the next one is a billion dollar exploit and they don't get enough donations to cover the hole

62184734
>>62184685
maybe...

i doubt AI's role in this though

62184749
>>62184487
Damn, skating is stressful

62184773
>>62184734
Have you been asleep for the last few months? Claude Mythos was a gamechanger and it's only going to accelerate from here

62184782
>>62184773
i doubt it.....

i understand security is an issue, and probably getting worse
i'm just saying i doubt AI as being crucial to this, in the present and the future

62184787
Sell property BC and ETH now!

62184807
>>62184782
Security experts from all over the world have been writing about finding more exploits with AI in 1-2 weeks than they had been able to find manually in their entire careers combined. Usually not crypto-related exploits, because the people who find those are smart enough to take advantage of them instead of publicly talking about them.

Are you scared of AI replacing your job or something? You seem to be in denial of what is actually happening

62184824
>>62184807
>Are you scared of AI replacing your job or something? You seem to be in denial of what is actually happening
i don't have a job & you seem very dedicated to this shtick...

62184827
>>62184824
I didnt realize you were a schizo. My bad

62184967
>>62184157
>even though that has been happening on an almost monthly basis for the past 2-3 years
And that's unironically a good thing.

Let all the weak links fail, only the strong ones remain.

62184985
>>62184487

62185157
How does 3% in crypto staking or say even 7-8% in an etf/sp500 even stay ahead of inflation, capital gains tax and maker fees/gas? I’m just a dumb zoomer who doesn’t remember or know anything, but the pack of chicken legs was $6 when I moved out of mummy’s in 2017 and now it’s $16. If the price of diesel doesn’t come down soon, food will have done a 4x since then.

Why shouldn’t a guy gamble wildly on the shitcoin casino instead of cutting your lifestyle to basically just lose money by the time you cash it out?

item.title: kek remember how scared boomers were when buffet wento into cash? — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
kek remember how scared boomers were when buffet wento into cash?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-03 23:28 UTC

OP

kek remember how scared boomers were when buffet wento into cash? surely the old wise master must've seen something coming
LMAO

Early replies (first 3)

62184553
>>62184535
buffett saw his retirement. the new guy will deploy the cash or be fired (i hope) by the shareholders.

bears here cling to anything including berkshires cash pile.

62184588
its going to be hard to compete without the insider informations buffet was getting from his jewish friends in the senate

fuck brka

62184590
>>62184535
Lmaooooooo
What happened to always bet on america?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184606
>>62184590
bro bet on bonds lol

62184616
>>62184588
RegFD redpill was the biggest aura loss I've ever witnessed.
I knew he was getting backroom deals because of his size, but this really destroyed the myth.
J. Boggle is my now my #1 fin boomer.

62184633
>>62184590
Bro bet on light skinnededass mocha light frappe ass looking N-Slurs and now he undapuffomin ongod

62185204
>>62184535
>when buffet went into cash
That's a misreading of events. BH has always held around 10%-15% cash, for a number of reasons.
a) they own a lot of stocks that pay dividends
b) they own some large insurance and reinsurance companies that may be subject to massive payouts if a hurricane hits the wrong part of the coast
c) they are frequently selling and buying stocks, in a company that large even slight differences in the rates of selling and buying can cause large fluctuations in their cash position
d) as the value of the company goes up, the value of their cash holdings also goes up, even though their cash position as a %age of the company's value remains roughly constant, yet retards will point at the dollar increase in cash position as significant and meaningful.

62185208
>>62185204
hes at 37% right now

i didnt read the rest of your post

62185239
>>62184535
Buffett's daddy was a jew.s. senator, so he's likely got the inside track...

62185241
>>62185208
Good. He sold high like a chad. The market is massively overvalued, when the crash happens BH is gonna make bank snapping up all those deals.

item.title: Gentrification of the entire world — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Gentrification of the entire world

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-03 23:23 UTC

OP

I remember seeing these 10 years ago when I was into boating. It's a hollow surfboard with a weedeater engine and a little jet drive so you can scoot around on the water. The inventor was showing off the 1st gen units, they cost something like $3k USD which I thought was expensive at the time, how could ordinary people afford that? My brain at the time couldn't comprehend just how fucked the future could possibly get.

These things now cost $10k+ euros,15k+ USD, $20k+ CAD. There's electric versions too, they should be cheap right? Just a battery and a motor, no, it's priced at $21k CAD.

Hoverboards and electric scooters weren't much of a thing at the time either, but china made them by the millions and made them cheap enough so that anyone could afford mobility. These guys went down the opposite route.

Something has gone very wrong with our civilization, instead of trying to make things as afforadble as possible so that everyone can have access to new experiences and a better quality of life which leads to a stronger community through shared culture, our companies have gone down the route of increasing prices to be as high as possible so a small handful of very rich people can enjoy life while the masses fade into irrelevance. In the past this business model would not have been sustainable because of a lack of capital, but now there's so much printed money floating around chasing returns that it seems that any kind of predatory business that overcharges for 'luxury' is viable.

How much longer can this continue?

Early replies (first 3)

62184907
>>62184899
who cares?

buy a chinese inflatable boat if your poor and leave the carbon fiber titanium 2 stroke jetsurfs for the elite

62184959
>>62184899
The top 10% do 50% of the consumption. Might as well cater everything to them.

62184976
>>62184907
This is what I mean, they make you think it's a premium luxury item by using marketing like carbon fiber and titanium. Neither of these materials are actually expensive or necessary. The cost in carbon fiber is mainly from the hand layup process, which on a part the size of a small surf board isn't much and you could just as easily use fiberglass like all marine stuff. And titanium while pricier than aluminum doesn't justify a $20,000 price tag and has no benefits in this particular application.

These boards should realistically cost about $1400 if they were mass manufactured but instead it's easier for them to keep it as a boutique product and sell way less of them for alot more money.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184978
>>62184899
they are free if you just steal one

62184982
>>62184899
>$3k USD which I thought was expensive
Just how poor are you? What special knowledge did you have at the time that convinced you that your opinion was more valid and informed than the actual manufacturers? And why do you think the world owes you access to every little frivolity that happens to catch your eyes at whatever price you think they should charge? Just how narcissistic are you, and did you take your meds today?
>These guys went down the opposite route.
Perhaps if you think *really* hard about the inherent differences between a scooter and a surfboard, the inherent differences between their use cases, as well as the sizes and demographics of their respective potential markets, you might realize the *really* *fucking* *obvious* differences between them that led their manufacturers on their opposite routes. Or just sit there and seethe ignorantly, I don't really care.

62185191
>>62184982
I bet I'm way richer than you if we're talking FIAT. The special knowledge of knowing how things are made and what prices mass market goods would bear, the business model of chasing only the ultra wealthy didn't exist for stuff like this at the time. You can make one of these by hand from scratch for $3-4k, even cheaper if you harvest an electric scooter, charging $20k shouldn't be possible in a real capitalist economy.

The inherent differences in the markets exist because of the pricing model, no one had electric scooters 15 years ago. I remember because I got one given to me for free, it was many thousands of dollars when new and ran off of lead acid batteries. Once the batteries died they threw it away because the technology was shit an a PITA to deal with. Hoverboards and escooters took off after it became cheap enough for the average person to afford them, before that it was mainly out of touch wealthy faggots on $$$ segways.

62185206
why would i care if some richfags or poorfags with credits wants to surf on the sea

you guys really need to grow up ffs also this board is not your personal blog fuck your mother

62185216
>>62185191
>You can make one of these by hand from scratch for $3-4k
Then make your own then, and stop your whining about how unfair life is that nobody is giving you one for free. You aren't special, everybody is their mommy's bestest little boy.

62185228
>>62185191
>charging $20k shouldn't be possible in a real capitalist economy
Serious question - just how stupid are you? Or are you a turdie, or a 14yo incel that's never left your mother's boyfriend's basement? Because those are the only 3 types of people that could so confidently make such an utterly and obviously wrong claim and not instantly die of shame.

item.title: >I keep having this dream >Sergey's pushing a log from his rump >I keep screaming, I don't want him to dump >"You're mak… — 9 replies — 14.7/hr

>I keep screaming, I don't want him to dump

>"Why?! Why are you dumping?!"

>"Sergey, you're lying!"
>"You always say that! You always say that this is the last dump!"
>"But you keep dumping more LINK! Sergey you cunt!!!"

>"Sneed, you're lying!"

62182276

62182306

:3

62182383

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 30 replies — 76.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-03 23:07 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184328
>>62184289
No he is right there really are no jobs. In 2019 u could send out 20 applications and get a 2 stage job and offer

now it takes 1k to maybe get one offer.

62184335
>>62184286
>Going for a master's degree when I can pay 20 dollars for a few minutes of token usage that will do a better job than you do with an 8 hour workday
If you don't have a white collar job by now it's time to start looking for a job in the real world.

62184344
>>62184315
I never understood the concept of dream company. You are a cubicle monkey in a department. Said department could have cancer leadership when you are and it’s miserable. You can’t say something is your dream company by looking at the marketing they put out

62184360
>>62184335
I have one but might get laid off. Just like every other white collar worker. Manual labor doesn't pay enough to live and will also get replaced so dont start with the seething. At least I'll get Hella unemployment. Gonna start a vending machine business or something. Anything to not have to toilet away for 18 bucks an hour like u

62184366
>>62184344
It was a quantum computing company. I would be in the lab, not a cubicle. Would have been surrounded by smart people. I went into engineering cuz I like it. Its crazy how most people have no actual interest in anything whatsoever, explines whu most of yall cant understand the concept of a dream job. Would i rather be piling up vicoritas secret models? Actually no beacuse math and engineering are fun but u get my poiint, its a realizstic drea. I was a shoe in for the role also. They just decided to cancle the whole thing outright. There were never trying to hire anyone.

62184396
>>62184360
>Manual labor doesn't pay enough to live
You can go work at a construction company and move cones for $20 an hour. Should be plenty since you probably invested your funds well while white collar work still existed.

62184406
>>62184335
it's mop mop mop flip mop mop mop dip of course.

62184410
Every dollar saved
I hardly spend
The economy is going to get crushed soon I feel it

62184411
>>62184396
Being homeless sounds more appealing

62184413
>>62184286
you suck and no one wants to pay for your work

US unemployment is 4%.... you are in the bottom 4%.

62184461
>>62184413
>doesn't understand what the unemployment figure measures.

based retard

62184496
>>62184413
fuck off retard EVERY JOB SECTOR is struggling with ghost jobs and unemployment

kys

62184529
>>62184413
Im currently employed but I haven't been looking for a new job for months cuz I sense a layoff

62184533
>>62184461
How do you interpret it? There is no way its 4 percent when not on person in any of my classes has an internship

62184556
>>62184286
I just got a job here last week
>90k+ salary
>IT Management role, ideally IT Director
>full time, salary, complete package of benefits

What you did was not question things and follow what the people who wanted you to be a grunt told you to do. What I did was self improve and do jobs that exposed me to alot of industries and people started seeking me out to get their problems solved. Do you see where you fucked up?
>Protip: College is a scam, companies will hire you over college normies if you know your shit and do good work, both of which a college student cant provide proof of outside "le meme certificate"
Reality is an IQ test, what do you think your IQ is?

62184660
>>62184556
OP here, if i quit my job 90k would be a horrible blow to my current finances as I consider myself financially destitute at 110k. Which is why im looking for a job. What happens when a guy that knows his shir and does good work AND has a college degree in your field?

62184677
>>62184315
Translation: Thank you for your data, which we will now sell to third parties. So long, suckers

62184690
>>62184660
someone who actually went to college should have learned to network with others, as that is the 2nd part you go to college for. Did you not network with anyone? Keep in mind I self studied and did projects that I was interested in, which employers value 100x more than college completion. Did you do any projects in College that you would put into a portfolio? You sound like you are in a high cost of living area. 90k to a low cost of living area gives me plenty of comfort room to do whatever and take care of all needs with plenty left over.
>knows his shit, does good work and college degree
If you have the first two, there should have bene people who are coming to you to have you do work. If this isnt the case, then you're overexaggerating yourself. Those who do good work and know their shit will never actually run into no jobs, unless their work is in an empty industry that has no demand. I didn't even interview, they knew they wanted me and hired me as soon as they could.
>What experiences have you had that defines you as knowing your shit AND does good work?

62185003
>>62184301
>All these interviews just happened to not like me
Yes. It's you, not them. You are the common factor in all of those rejections.
>i had skills for the past 10 years
You skill - such as it was - was being a warm body to fill a seat for the bean counters who conflated head count for productivity (because that's the sort of category error that accountants always make). That skill is no longer particularly valuable.

62185045
>>62184690
My freind did oxy for 10 years, got sober, lied on his resume and passed a compTA. He's a networking engineer now. U arent special. Ill just take ur job when I fully give up

62185047
>>62184496
Yeah 4% unemployment, roughly the same level of unemployment as it's been the last 2 decades. There are plenty of jobs out there, and plenty of people getting hired into those jobs. I changed jobs just last year, made one call, had one interview, and got an offer before I walked out the door. 18% raise from my previous position. But I'm actually good at what I do, it's not just some delusion that I use to prop up my persecution complex.

So yes, it really is a skill issue, and you should git gud, you fucking faggot.

62185053
>>62185003
You mis read what I said on purpose. Since they reposted the job in a bootlicker retard world like urs that would mean that no one they interviewed was good enough. Which is insane of course. Why do u bootlicker so hard? I was a bootlicker just like you and it got me nowhere, you will find our soon enough zoomie

62185055
>>62185047
How do yoy explines emails like this?>>62184315
Im employed now but im not gonna larp as if everything is okay. Have fun getting laid off alongside me in a few months

62185058
>>62185047
Also. I got a job too last year, that was last year retard. Shit has changed, u retard.


Also if u were better than everyone else, then you would realize the exception doesnt make the rule.

62185125
>>62185053
>no one they interviewed was good enough
Yes. It happens a lot and this isn't a recent phenomenon as you seem to believe. I've seen it happen from the other side, you get a tranche of candidates in to interview and none of them are suitable, maybe one guy has the chops but has an attitude problem, another guy turns out to be a good bullshitter, another guy got good grades in his classes but doesn't seem to remember a thing about what was covered in them, &c. So you put out the position again.
>>62185058
>the exception doesnt make the rule.
You should think very hard about that before making a fool of yourself on the internet. 4% unemployment. *You* are the exception. *You* and a very few other people are undesireable. It's not a conspiracy against you (no matter how better that makes you feel), it is your fault that you are having trouble getting a job.
>>62185055
Positions do close without being filled. It could be worse, he could have been hired for the job, quit his current job, then get laid off before ever getting his first paycheck. It happened to a junior coworker of mine back in 1992, he was doing the junior job-hopping thing climbing the salary ladder and hopped right off a cliff. He was able to get back his previous job at our company because we had started interviewing but hadn't filled it yet, but took a pay cut because he was desperate and we knew he was gonna job hop again at the first opportunity. And yes all those prospective interviewees got their rejection letter and a notice that the position was closed. Shit happens.

62185134
>>62185058
>Shit has changed
It really hasn't, you're just too young and naive to realize it. Nihil sub sole novum.

62185164
>>62184286
just wait. they is doing a stimulus soon.

item.title: Old guy in Corvette cope — 69 replies — 23.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Old guy in Corvette cope

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-05-03 20:36 UTC

OP

This will be YOU, just some cringe old fart finally getting his dream car after muh 401k investing for 40 years.

If you're not investing everything into Bitcoin RIGHT NOW to potentially make it in 10 years while you still have some youth left it's fucking over for you. I hope I make it live like a fucking king in my youth while you will be waging till 60 years old.

MAKE IT OR DIE

Early replies (first 3)

62181483
i would rather buy a corvette over buying bitcoin at 78K

62181491
>>62181462

No I bought my dream car 3 years ago and it got repossessed and emptied my 401k and have to declare bankruptcy. I'm only 35 though.

62181498
are we just glossing over the fact there's a fucking dinosaur in the background?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181855
>>62181462
Nah I'm a toyota man. GM = garbage machines. I do like the old camaros though

62181861
>>62181572
literally rental vibes, they day of showing off wealth via cars is over, you should just buy the most fun car thats it

62181905
Should I buy this 2020 C-Class? I've got a toddler, but the stroller should fit in a back.

62181907
>>62181905

62182125
>>62181462
>investing? pffft I just buy thousands of lottery tickets with my disposable income
kys
yeah, it'd be nice to be rich at 25 but most of us weren't born to rich parents so it is what it is

62182131
>>62181572
If you're ugly women won't care and guys will say you're coping
if you're hot and handsome women will fuck you in a crack den and guys will envy you
if you care about what others think this isn't really the place for you

62182471
>>62181462
It will be me
But that car is a diesel mitsubishi delica

62182481
>>62181462
Already have 350 BTC, I'm one bullrun away from private jet money

62182517
>>62181462
the c8 looks cool but how can I drive a fucking automatic as a dream car? come on. I can't. I won't. That shit is gay. I'd rather just get a cooler golf cart for my 55+ community going to HOA meetings and shit. maybe one that has fiberglass that makes it look like an Escalade? do they make those? I'm not driving a fast automatic C8 fuck off with that I'll probably crash anyway. I'll take the primo golf cart thanks

62182527
>>62181782
this

62182532
>>62181491
nice buddy, real nice. A man of culture.

I was thinking about starting 72t SEPP distributions on my retirement IRA but once you turn it on you, you have to keep it distributing, right? What if I finally got a job? Would I be able to stop it?

62183177
My dream daily driver car is a 2025 lexus es300h ultra luxury. I believe I can mod it for $80k total (including car msrp) to within spitting distance of a rolls Royce in comfort and silence, minus the opulent bullshit and the ostentatiousness. My dream car was originally a rolls Royce, but it has bmw electronics so they can spy on you, no self driving, and it isn't stealthy enough. I don't want people to know I'm rich.
My first Lexus choice was the ls500h, but it's too big.
I wouldn't buy any depreciating asset on a loan, so I would fully pay for my car.

62183180
>>62181462
>till 60
Hahhahaha 80 you mean

62183185
>>62181519
yeah arizona just has them

62183189
>>62181544
>still a lot of car
is this the biggest self report of all time?

62183194
>>62181619
>work from home
im doing a survey of sorts. what are you doing that allows you to WFH after covid?

62183247
>>62181462
I’ve been driving a corvette since my late 20’s and I’m in my mid 30’s now. Skill issue.

62183296
>>62183194
IT

62183330
>>62181619
>460K
no one cares about your poorfag rubles

62183336
>>62183330
what a jewish thing to say.
if you are any race but jewish you should be ashamed of yourself. Wake up anon.

62183345
>>62181498
Don't worry, it's behind a fence.

62183408
>>62181462
This is literally that boomer wojak where he's like
>now that I'm in my 50s and completed all my life goals, I can finally get a gf

62183637
>>62181462
I retired at 24 and are back to waging at 36, not for long though.

62183644
If you dont drive a v8, lower your tone when talking to me

62183659
>>62183330
460K euros is almost 1M dollars, mutto

62184540
>>62181462
I have 15k in MSTR, bitcoin maxi chud, tell me, what will that be worth in 10 years ?

62184625
>>62181905
no

62184639
>>62181498
holy shit is that real

62184698
>>62183644
>V8
Pussy shit. Make it money is having a butler to drive you around with the V10. Touching a steering wheel is for the poor.

62184707
>rental
Few years pass and you would have already done a huge chunk of the payments.
RENTKEKS ARE SUBHUMANS

item.title: Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET? — 189 replies — 22.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 189 • Updated: 2026-05-03 15:02 UTC

OP

Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62158601
>>62158557
Neet
Wagie can get some money to go out and have fun, and socialize.
might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

62158623
>>62158601
>might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

while waging at walmart is impossible

62158626
>>62158557
I prefer to be a neet, but ofc neither situation is perfect. Now if i was getting pussy then we'd be talking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62171998
>>62158557
NEETs have dignity.

62175198
>>62158601
Skill issue. I'm working on become turboneet through three sources and will clear 93-95k when they are approved.

62175347
>>62170481
>>62170345
Almost everyone that owns a house in a top tier city came from well off families. I don’t think the majority truly realize how important family help is and how big the wealth gap is.
Good houses in good areas are truly unaffordable for normal or people in the bottom 95%.

62175361
>>62170497
> Either has their pros and cons. Waging can get enraging and demoralizing but is a wonderful source of social, schedule, productivity, and income. Neeting has freedom and self-expression but can get so isolating and repetitive that it can make you break down mentally entirely over time.
There is nothing productive about waking up at 6am everyday. Neet is a choice but waging isn’t. I think this is what no one wants to say. No one working would choose to work if they didn’t have to work.

62175389
>>62158557
Diogenes, Buddha and Jesus was Neets
nobody remembers the trillions of wagies
being a wagie is worser than being a neet

62175537
>>62167410
the real best of both worlds is WFH wagie
>forced into a regular wake up schedule that isn’t obscenely early or late
>no commute
>less oversight allows you to better enjoy the downtime inherent to any job, without giving you so much free time as to burn out media quickly
>access to your own kitchen during breaks and all the wagie bucks to buy good ingredients
>once again no commute, allowing you to instantly switch to leisure the moment the workday is done

62176675
>>62168012
https://soul-zone.org/

62177246
>>62158601
40 years ago maybe

62177573
>>62168700
>when a girl you just saw is now CRAZY into you, to the point of saying she loved you, keep trying to makeout with you, is just all over you
pointless to think about because it has never happened and will never happen

62177728
neets arent masculine and no one respects them

62179485
At least neets get to sleep.

62179529
neets because there's nothing like realizing you wasted your whole damn life and also you're a lazy piece of shit that should've just killed yourself

62179583
>>62179529
i genuinely don't know whether you're talking about wagies or neets

62179651
>>62168700
> social media lied to me
> i didn’t participate in it
> mfw
I’m not saying you’re choosing wrong, not everyone is suited to NEET life and the world needs productive people.
But maybe just have some faith in yourself that you were making your own bad choices and then turned it around when you found motivation.

62179923
After having neet'maxxd and wage cage'd and finally landed at a job I actually don't mind that pays very well the last is infinitely preferable to both of the former, but I'd put NEET above any true wagie job. I see some people act like a mundane office job is wagie work and I just assume they've never had to work in a truly dead end hellscape position. There are some truly soul-crushing jobs out there.

62179934
>>62165202
Being able to show up at the same time every day is a skill. Dealing with people is a skill.

62179952
>>62166566
It's ok anon. You picked yourself up. I fucked off in my 20s. I wasn't a neet but I have nothing to show for it. Didn't save any money. Thankfully I did enough to get into a good law school and I just graduated at 33. But it does suck being years behind people who are younger than me and making partner. However you have years and years of career ahead of you. Don't look back.

62179958
>>62166748
People who had parents who can leave an inheritance worth anything are generally successful. You're a crab.

62180244
>>62179934
Seriously. Going to work everyday day is a skill. So tempting to just take a day off.

62180261
>>62177246
>>62177573
Ok guys
I gonna go tonight again.
and next day.
If I didn’t end up making out with a hot a girl like last time, I will get back here and tell you it might have been a fluke
>>62179651
I said that because tons of guys are also getting blasted by social media and just gave up

62180686
>>62180261
Alright night one reporting in
Fail
On to night 2 tomorrow

62181149
>>62158557
Neet
>t. Neet
If you're a wagie at least you can have a social life and if it's something like working at a bar you can easily meet girls too

62181917
>>62181149
lol
lmao

62181937
>>62179923
Give examples of some soul crushing jobs. What dream job did you land?

62183586
>>62158557
I've been both a NEET and a wagie. I prefer the wagie life because it gave me the money to buy the things I like and because it forced me to grow my persona by facing difficult situations and interacting with various kind of people

62183593
>>62183586
>forced me to grow my persona by facing difficult situations and interacting with various kind of people
NEETS laugh at this as a defense mechanism for having arrested development, unable to make eye contact

62183615
>>62180261
Either way, falling for the trap of "I was brainwashed into my decisions" is retard behavior and doesn't lead to improving your life

62183617
>>62183593
Being an introvert with superior levels of grey matter and scientific proof social interaction drains my energy isn't "cope" it's facts

62183824
>>62181937
>Give examples of some soul crushing jobs
Jobs with high death risk or very taxing to health

62183862
>>62158557
Probably wagie but it kinda depends on your job.
Believe it or not there are cool jobs out there that even a vast amount of wealth can scarcely recreate.
Being one of these adventurous cunts who sails the oceans on a cargo ship for example. Battling against tall waves and somali pirates, stopping in foreign countries and getting the opportunity to travel around for a couple days, trying the local food, watching containers fall overboard as the ship goes fucking 45 degrees sideways in a storm. The works.

That being said, I'd take NEETdom over having to flip burgers or work a register all day. And I'd take a bullet over ever having to wash dishes ever again.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-03 22:48 UTC

62182548

62182571

62182576
>>62182571

62182593
isnt their chip the whole fucking 300mm wafer. yield is gonna be total dogshit

62182607
>>62182593

62182962
hmmm interesting

62182990
>>62182535

62183027
>>62182990

62184653

62185092

62185108

item.title: The LINK token has value — 54 replies — 15.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-05-03 20:00 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176418
>>62176415
Don't reply to me ever again ranjeesh.

62176477
do stinkies even understand that pain and misery are LITERALLY their only friends

62176567
>saaars do not listen to the fuddergods, link token has value ssars! link token needed! do not redeem!

kek, who has ever even ehard of the bitwise

62176598
>>62176477
You’ve been posting here multiple times a day using the same formula for over a year. The style is played out. Aren’t you bored? Clearly you’re not very smart or inspired. You’re not dissuading the newfag or making anyone sell, so what are you doing? All you’re doing is showing the world how pathetic you are. Try something new, I promise it will feel good.

62176740
Is it march already? Weren't we supposed to be pumping in march???

62176756
I'm just going to hold until all tokens are distributed. Is that okay with you?

62176892
>>62176477
Welcome to another Chainlink thread
Be nice!

62176910
>>62176284
I dumped LINK and I moved into BTC. I started staking some recently and might start lending too with TBV now becoming more relevant. Will I make it with just BTC?

62176913
>>62176910
will you make it if what you're holding does a 2x?

62176989
>>62176914
What date is this video from?

62177088
>>62176295
Shartemis betray

62177113
>>62176284
>>62176298
>>62176344
>>62176415
>>62176477

62177436
>>62176989
2025. In the video they're expecting good things from 2026

62177765
>>62177436
I have expected good things for many years now. I'm still a believer but not a short term believer. Over the mid to long term I still think we'll win but it's definitely gonna be 10 years further down the line than we hoped for in 2018. In 2018 I'd said 2023 would be the year. But 3 years after that I don't see much in terms of price action, and who knows about actual adoption when the above video is still talking about trials, even if they are live trials.

62177825
>>62177765
at least they stopped calling them proof of concepts. bagholders really didn't like that term anymore. trials is also played out, so live trials it is. maybe they can call it "limited production usage" next, what do you think? would you like that term?

62177889
>>62176214
value like a new car has value, sure..

62177980
>>62176214
Only 273,000,000 LINK tokens left to dump before the team start dumping the reserve tokens. LOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOL. Get fucked stinkies

62177984
>>62177980
My ID is real fr no cap

62178008
>>62177825
Zach and that insufferable faggot, nullpackets, have been yapping about "live pre-production" for the past 2 years
it's truly hilarious

62178052
>>62178008
Ever since their breadcrumb boy at SWIFT stepped down there has been literally not a single shred of good news for the stinkers.

62178211
chainlink is the plumbing of the new finance world, and Link is the shit that flows through the pipes

on the other end of those pipes are link holders, whom sit with mouths open as receptacles to collect, drink, and shower in said shit

they do this with a smile on their face. why you ask? well, because, they are....

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62178228
just admit you lost the game of life, stinkies

62178833
>>62178052
>seething at Tom

Cripple detected

62179742
>>62177984
your id has fboy in it, that is really funny
>>62178228
thanks for posting in the Chainlink thread
>>62177088
How? There's nothing for them to betray, they posted speculation then left
>>62177765
>I have expected good things for many years now.
That's good, meaning the company has been running strong for years, meaning the it's not a scam, meaning the number is going up over time

62181961
>>62176214
True, it's 9$, kek

62182305
>>62179742
I don't think it's a scam at all. I'm just unhappy with the price action. Otherwise, the company seems to be doing great.

62182805
>>62181961
and the number keeps going up!
>>62182305
that's your valid opinion!

62184151
Chainlink powering prediction markets, the numbers are going up

62184641
>>62176214
the revenue is increasing

item.title: The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. — 62 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-05-03 18:23 UTC

OP

The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy
Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. It is the accumulated result of tax cuts without matching reductions in spending, an aging population drawing more heavily on Social Security and Medicare, and years of political leadership on both sides of the aisle that has consistently chosen short-term popularity over long-term solvency.
You fags are cooked (including me)

Early replies (first 3)

62179629
Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt

Refute this without sounding mad

62179630
>>62179622
breaking news: voters want stuff and dont want to pay for it

62179853
>>62179622
AI will fix this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181803
>>62181798

this is how bankers roll

62181805
>>62181803
xd

62181821
>>62179629
on g_d?

62181849
>>62179622
>>62181803

time to buy

62181852
>>62181849

more

62182923
>>62179928
The US is a shitty nation that was a former British colony, you're not better than India. Not even 300 years of history and you're not certainly an Empire. Go fuck yourselves nigger lovers

62182943
>>62179629
And the US government cannot collect from me lol

62182953
>>62179629
I invest in Bank of China. Even if the rest of world purchase in Yuan, I profit.

62182956
>>62179622
>kslop penis mounters image
F I L T E R E D
I
L
T
E
R
E
D

62182970
>>62182923
you sound mad. you don't want Orange Man to pull more troops out of your poor country do you? go get your shine box

>>62179928
nobody's taking shit

>>62179622
still the best game in town. rage harder poor countries, US debt you will still buy, still want, and you'll continue to lend to the US

62183031
>>62180399
Those 41% are probably all aging boomers who couldn't fight anyway though.

62183049
>>62179928
>nobody has ever invaded the United States.
Exactly, Saar.

62183104
>>62179629
>because no one can collect from the US Govt
The Federal Reserve already collects from the US government. The US is only solvent as long as they do the Feds bidding.

62183137
>>62179928
>300 million all of a sudden joining together because of some anon's stupid chest-pounding fantasy
>t. retard who's been somehow asleep the past year

62183141
>>62179622
All you have to do is cut to 2019 spending levels and we would have a surplus.
That's how crazy Congressional spending has increased since Covid.

62184058
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aDAL5G9Xwn4&list=RDaDAL5G9Xwn4
https://i.4cdn.org/biz/1777749403133051.png

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4ZHwu0uut3k&list=RD4ZHwu0uut3k
>[720p]
https://i.4cdn.org/biz/1777749468586523.jpg

62184159
>>62179629
Most of the US debt isn't from foriegn countries. It's US themselves. US Pension & retirement funds, US investment firms, US etfs etc. If US defaults you'll see US citizens and companies lose money. Holding US treasuriers aren't going to be worth as much since US isn't going to meet its obligations. Companies and people will lose a lot money. Contraction of the economy will happen for years due to this cycle. You'll also see a stock market crash and the decades of growth will vanish because nobody will want to lend to the US at similar rates. It'll probably be as bad as the great depression if not worse.


Hopefully this happens and I can a US citizen as a slave wagie.

62184174
>>62180399
This is not a survey of people willing to fight for their country; it's a survey of people willing to click "yes" on an internet poll. The reality would be a tiny tiny fraction of that number.

62184182
>>62184174
Dude we literally go to europe and beat the fuck out of your pickpocket niggers and muslims if they fuck with us. Realistically only 5 million Americans would be needed to beat europe down like dogs

62184190
>>62179629
The US depends on people continuing to buy its debt, just to fund ongoing expenses

62184198
>>62180442
>eliminate all taxes for people making less than ~75k a year
Uh, lower taxes won't help the debt.

>block immigration
More people = more tax revenue

62184199
>>62184190
Just like all country?

62184207
>>62184199
There are countries that run small or no deficits and could cope quite well with zero net debt issuance.

62184218
>>62180410
india has like 1.4 billion people the self-hating white obsessed ones you see in the west represent a tiny fraction the actual ones in india are all retarded jingoists or muslims

62184243
>>62184182
That's cute but every single time in the past 30 years that you guys have had an opportunity to show that you're not all talk, you've rolled over like bitches. Off the top of my head:
>allowing cops to go door to door confiscating guns during katrina
>allowing military to go door to door conducting warrantless searches after the boston marathon bombing
>hiding in your house posting on twitter while mobs of leftists took over entire cities while completely crippling police forces and burning down police stations during the george floyd riots
You lives are too comfortable to ever risk them. You have fun larping online but we all know that the only people willing to risk their lives in the real world are those with either strong convictions or little to lose and we both know that you have a lot to lose and don't really believe in anything.

The only time Americans show any balls is when there's a call for people to die for Israel.

62184279
>>62179622
>>62179629
>Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt
>>62179928
>Come and take the debt
funny to see americans think the debt is owed to other countries instead of its own people.

62184305
>>62184279
forgot pic related

62184336
>>62179622
>Cut defense spending
>Cut medicare
>Cut social security
>Tax rich people, tax the middle class, tax the working class, tax the poor, tax your mom. Tax your Grandma. Everyone's getting picked up, flipped over around shaken for money and needs to get over it.
>Inflate the currency
>Keep every other program because those those don't actually matter that much. In fact, add more.
>Ignore culture wars. Ignore culture clowns.
>Pay our doomer, meme-brained, low IQ, lazy and angertainment-addicted public a few pennies to go to the park to end the loneliness epidemic and polarization and just go outside and see how human beings function
>Cut the "muh affordability" meme subsidizes. We're adults. Show them the real bill and just stop it.
>Nuclear, Wind and Solar power. Shut up about the rest.
>Viciously murder zoning, car centricity and nimby laws on public television
Amendment guaranteeing that you have to let people build houses on land they buy provided it's not a safety or environmental hazard.
>Respect law outcomes, but persecute each and every corrupt official no matter what to where you make Watergate look like a peace rally compared it.
>Expose every grifter. They might not be prosecutable, but they will be known.
>Spew TRVTHS daily and nightly no matter who it hurts (including yourself)
There. Fixed your country.

Do you think the average American won't do this because they're afraid won't work or because we are afraid of seeing the infeasibility and sunk cost of the current American lifestyle?

62184343
>>62184207
Such as?

62184400
>>62179629
Eventually the dollar collapses though and bond yields spike because people demand higher and higher interest on what's a collapsing dollar

item.title: Why does /biz hate Pokemon? I've literally told many of you for YEARS to invest in it but you all still think it's just… — 65 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why does /biz hate Pokemon? I've literally told many of you for YEARS to invest in it but you all still think it's just…

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-05-03 18:07 UTC

OP

Why does /biz hate Pokemon? I've literally told many of you for YEARS to invest in it but you all still think it's just a stupid card game for children.

Early replies (first 3)

62181434
>>62181426
No I rather not sit on ebay all day getting messages from browns trying to haggle the prices down. Easier and more convient ways to make money, this shit will have so many bagholders just like the meme sport cards

62181436
I have a box of pokemon cards that I had when I was a kid, are they worth anything or just the rare shiny card in absolute peak mint condition?

62181438
>>62181426
How can shiny plastic cards cost so much? Even if they're limited in supply, how can there exist so much demand for them?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62183294
>>62183004
Here's the thing about pokemon "collectors" most of them don't stay in the hobby for more than 5 years, most of them are just passing through. If you actually understood the history of the pokemon market, and participated in it outside of the relatively recent booms, you'd know that current valuations are all speculative hype. I'm just a humble autist who collected before these speculative retards entered the space, and I've netted quite the hefty profit off of their naive investment thesis.

62183306
>>62181426
>Muh physical bing bing wahoo NFTs
Lol, lmao even.

62183308
>>62181438
Try actually selling though - it's an illiquid market.

62183377
>>62183306
>He keeps dealing in the real world instead of monetizing bing bing wahoos
Kek, ngmi even.

62183400
>>62183191
>actual tangible
That does nothing because it's a children's toy, you fucking idiot. Cryptocoins like Hype next year or Avantis last year or Aero in 2024 went the distance and minted millionaires because they have utility within the crypto ecosystems whether that be RWAs, Perpdexes or Basechain, but you're too much of a retarded scammer faggot to understand the difference between something like SNDK, a stock which deals in NAND DRAM 10xing in 8 months and your little worthless beanie baby bubble because like I said in your head youv'e removed even the veneer of this shit being useful and probably are upset because you invested in dogcoins or BAYC in 2021 because you're not a serious person.

62183413
>>62183400
> Cryptocoins like Hype next year or Avantis last year or Aero in 2024
> because you're not a serious person.

I’ll take Pokémon over this garbage any day of the week.

62183417
>>62181426
an article like this = top is in.

look for the next thing

62183441
>>62181426
i assume that only english and japanese cards are worth something? i got a vendor machine where you can buy german packs but nobody uses it

62183444
>>62181426
>it's just a stupid card game for children.
correct

62183488
where am i supposed to sell 4 of these things without getting scammed?

62183494
You are unironically not bullish enough on Nintendo. See past the fud. See how they already are the next Disney and will control the entertainment industry for the next 15 years and at least for the next 100 years.

62183720
I collect magic cards
I find the game fun and me and my friends play together every few weeks.
Its a comfy investment but not a great one.
pokemon seems lonely since no one actually plays the game.

62183729
>>62183016
Yeah and the art market is totally controlled by a bunch of NY Jews.

62183736
>>62183488
Any local card shop will buy that due to its rarity, just keep calling up all the ones in your area. Goddamn I still feel sick to the stomach thinking how many of those I opened back when it was still cheap.

62183741
>>62183494
They've almost surpassed Disney already.

62183742
>>62183736
i dont wanna sell for half price though. card shops are all run by jews that will look you in the eye and shake your hand as they lie to you and rape you
>ebay buyers will scam me
>fb marketplace buyers will jump me and rob me
>card shops will rape me
>not gonna sit at a table at a convention and talk to 8 year olds and their single dads all day either
i think im just fucked

62183751
>>62183742
Good, don't sell it because it's just going to keep going up in value anyway. In 10 years it'll probably be worth almost $10k anyway.

62183755
>>62183751
this is the top
https://x.com/ashrobin/status/2050649139185115368

62183776
>>62183755
He's talked about Pokemon for years though, that's nothing new. The absolute peak will probably be this September when the 30th anniversary set comes out. After that will probably be a cool off period but Pokemon always comes back up over time.

62183785
>>62183082
I couldn't imagine being a legal assistant. That shit is gay.

t. Lawyer

62183787
>>62183776
thats ai slop
but yeah youre right it cant die before the anniversary
is there info on the real 30th anniversary set yet?

62183886
>>62181438
It's basically fallout bottlecap money.

62183943
>>62181426
Nigga this went 5x in one year, the only position you should have now is short position.

62183951
>>62181426
My very first business idea as a little kid was a website for tabulating the rarity + prices of pokemon cards
My dad talked me out of it since he thought there would be no demand and that I'd have no way of finding out all the rarities and keeping up with it all by myself
I hope I don't accidentally discourage my kids from their ideas with how hard they'll be to actualize before they actually give it an honest try

62184077
>>62183787
nothing yet, TPC only reveals info on new sets a few weeks before it actually comes out

>>62183951
Ouch, you missed out badly and your dad is a dumbass boomer. That's what tcgplayer is now.

62184084
>>62181426
Have you managed to sell at a profit? Like big money 20k or more.
I suspect it suffers a lot from liquidity specially if you are not in USA.

62184131
>>62183046
No you think that you have 200k worth. I can sell my Bitcoin right now you have to go through 20 hoops and talk to brown subhumans all day trying to haggle you down. No thanks

62184152
ai slop doing better job than those pokechink

62184158
>>62184131
I can touch my pokemon cards right now you have to rely on a distributed system filled with scammers and hopeless narratives.

62184359
>>62183494
I got an unopened regular new 3ds xl. Looks like they are selling for $900 on fleebay. I paid about $150 on sale a long as time ago.

Who is buying this? I should probably just sell now right. The battery in it is probably useless now but I guess whoever buys it wouldn't open it either
